steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Half of $6$ is $3$, and $3^2=9$. Add and subtract $9$ to keep the expression equivalent.
$$x^2+6x+4=x^2+6x+9+4-9$$

steps.title: Rewrite the expression
steps.content:
The first three terms form a perfect square, and the constants combine to $-5$.
$$x^2+6x+4=(x+3)^2+4-9$$
$$(x+3)^2+4-9=(x+3)^2-5$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing the original expression and $(x+3)^2-5$ gives the same curve, so the rewritten expression is equivalent. The answer is B.
